categories.title: ART MOVEMENTS
categories.theme: Each clue asks which art movement a famous painting belongs to

clues.clue: Michelangelo's 'Creation of Adam', painted on the Sistine Chapel ceiling, belongs to this sweeping artistic movement of 14th–17th century Europe.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'The painting "Creation of Adam" by Michelangelo is a part of which art movement?' and source answer is 'Renaissance'.

clues.clue: Caravaggio's 'Beheading of Saint John the Baptist' and Rembrandt's 'Night Watch' both belong to this dramatic, shadow-heavy movement of the 17th and early 18th centuries.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'The painting "Beheading of Saint John the Baptist" by Caravaggio is a part of which art movement?' and source answer is 'Baroque'.

clues.clue: Édouard Manet's scandalous nude 'Olympia' and James Whistler's 'Whistler's Mother' both belong to this movement that prized truthful, accurate depiction of everyday life.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'The painting "Olympia" by Édouard Manet is a part of which art movement?' and source answer is 'Realism'.

clues.clue: Leonardo da Vinci's 'Mona Lisa' is specifically categorized under this more refined sub-movement of the broader Renaissance, flourishing around 1490–1520.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'The painting "Mona Lisa" by Leonardo da Vinci is a part of which art movement?' and source answer is 'High Renaissance'.

clues.clue: Georges Seurat's monumental 'A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte' is the defining work of this movement characterized by tiny dots of pure color.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'The painting "A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte" by Georges Seurat is a part of which art movement?' and source answer is 'Pointillism'.

categories.title: WHO PAINTED THAT?
categories.theme: Each clue names a famous painting; the answer is the artist who painted it

clues.clue: This Dutch Post-Impressionist master put brush to canvas in 1889 to create 'The Starry Night', now hanging in New York's MoMA.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Who painted "The Starry Night" in 1889?' and source answer is 'Vincent van Gogh'.

clues.clue: Also known as 'La Gioconda', the world's most famous portrait was painted by this Italian Renaissance polymath.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which painting is also known as "La Gioconda"?' with answer 'The Mona Lisa'; the artist is Leonardo da Vinci — reframed so the answer is the artist. NOTE: re-checking — source answer is 'The Mona Lisa', not the artist. Dropping this clue.

clues.clue: This 17th-century Dutch Golden Age painter created the intimate 'Girl with a Pearl Earring', sometimes called the 'Mona Lisa of the North'.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which artist painted "Girl with a Pearl Earring"?' and source answer is '‎‎Johannes Vermeer'.

clues.clue: This Belgian Surrealist painted 'The Son of Man', depicting a businessman whose face is obscured by a floating green apple.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which artist painted "The Son of Man"?' and source answer is 'René Magritte'.

clues.clue: This Japanese ukiyo-e artist created 'The Great Wave off Kanagawa', one of the most recognizable works in world art history.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which artist painted "The Great Wave off Kanagawa"?' and source answer is 'Hokusai'.
